headlines.title: Ann Widdecombe suspect not known to Prevent as counter terrorism police take over investigation
headlines.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood confirmed the suspect was not known to the government's anti-terrorism programme in an update to MPs.

headlines.title: Mason: Widdecombe's death renews debate over protection of politicians
headlines.description: Many MPs blame social media for normalising violent language and abuse, the BBC's political editor writes.

headlines.title: No significant rain forecast for at least a week as UK heatwave continues
headlines.description: Heat has been this big story of the summer so far but there has also been a lack of wet weather, as Ben Rich explains.

headlines.title: The mysterious crypto firm backed by Farage's biggest donor
headlines.description: Tether is a giant in crytocurrency, an industry that Nigel Farage believes the UK should be embracing.

headlines.title: Who will win the World Cup? The view from the semi-finalists' countries
headlines.description: BBC Sport speaks to journalists and fans from the World Cup's four semi-finalists to see who they think will win the tournament.

headlines.title: Australian police reveal unseen photos 25 years after British backpacker murder
headlines.description: Police hope the images could jog memories that will help them find the body of Peter Falconio.

headlines.title: YouTube still recommending eating disorder videos to teens, research finds
headlines.description: The site is continuing to recommend harmful videos despite new rules meant to better protect teenagers in the UK, a study finds.

headlines.title: Train crash driver died from traumatic injuries
headlines.description: An inquest hears Shaun Burton died in the collision in which 102 people needed hospital treatment.

headlines.title: Free school meal pupils falling further behind their peers, report warns
headlines.description: The Education Policy Institute says the new prime minister should target the issue with "laser-like focus".

headlines.title: Dad who died helping two children in sea named
headlines.description: A dad who died helping two children swimming at Seaton Carew beach is named locally as Wayne Taylor.

headlines.title: Lindsey Graham's sister, who he adopted as a child, to replace him as senator
headlines.description: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

headlines.title: England to make late call on Rice for semi-final
headlines.description: England will make a late decision on whether Declan Rice is fit to start Wednesday's World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

headlines.title: Time for Lamine Yamal to ignite? Or another Mbappe masterclass?
headlines.description: France face Spain for a place in the World Cup final on Tuesday, meaning a showdown between Kylian Mbappe and Lamine Yamal. Who comes out on top?

headlines.title: Increased security for England and Argentina tie
headlines.description: Authorities say there will be increased security measures in place for Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina in Atlanta.

headlines.title: How my period is supercharging my ADHD
headlines.description: A first-of-its-kind study by UK researchers is looking at the link between menstrual cycles and ADHD.

headlines.title: How to navigate the awkward year-end teacher 'thank you' collection
headlines.description: Collecting money for a joint present might overcome the competitive gift-giving, but it can still cause friction.

headlines.title: Andy Serkis defends lack of diversity in Lord of the Rings cast
headlines.description: Kate Winslet, Jamie Dornan and Anya Taylor-Joy are among the stars who have been cast so far.

headlines.title: I hunted the men who secretly film their wives and share footage online
headlines.description: What disturbed Jess the most was men sharing footage of wives, girlfriends and loved ones.

headlines.title: Hours each day in an iron lung kept her breathing, her will to live kept her alive
headlines.description: The sister of the last known polio patient in the US to use the life-saving machine spoke to the BBC about her sister's life.

headlines.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
headlines.description: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

headlines.title: US judge voids Donald Trump's $1.8bn settlement with IRS that gave him immunity from tax audits
headlines.description: The judge said the suit was brought for 'improper purposes' and referred a Trump attorney for possible disciplinary action.

headlines.title: Burnham cements Labour leadership with backing of 349 MPs
headlines.description: The MP for Makerfield is on track to become the prime minister next week after receiving the support of the vast majority of Labour MPs.

headlines.title: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
headlines.description: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

headlines.title: Bangkok fire investigation finds locked doors and flammable decor as deaths climb to 30
headlines.description: Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of signage marking emergency exits.

headlines.title: Identity of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 child abuse offences revealed
headlines.description: Hamish Tait is accused of abusing 136 children at five childcare facilities over a 16-year period.

headlines.title: Gang feud ended with 'resolution of differences' between rivals - police
headlines.description: Chief Constable Jo Farrell noted the feud - which spanned six months and involved 84 separate incidents - had ended for multiple reasons including dozens of arrests.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Counter Terrorism Police Investigating Ann Widdecombe Death
headlines.description: Counter terrorism police takeover the investigation into Ann Widdecombe’s death.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Mitch McConnell breaks his silence, will it stop the conspiracy theories?
headlines.description: A photo of the US senator has gone viral after he hadn’t been seen for weeks

headlines.title: Who will win the World Cup? The view from the semi-finalists
headlines.description: BBC Sport speaks to journalists and fans from the World Cup's four semi-finalists to see who they think will win the tournament.

headlines.title: Increased security for England v Argentina tie in Atlanta
headlines.description: Authorities say there will be increased security measures in place for Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina in Atlanta.

headlines.title: Could it work if Verstappen wanted to go to McLaren? F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your latest questions before the Belgian Grand Prix at Spa-Francorchamps.

headlines.title: Why it's time for The Open to crown an English winner
headlines.description: It is nearly 60 years since Tony Jacklin became the last Englishman to win The Open on English soil but there's every chance that drought may be broken this week, writes Iain Carter.

headlines.title: Timing took me to Chelsea rather than Liverpool - Alonso
headlines.description: Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso says his arrival at Stamford Bridge instead of Liverpool was "about timings".
